text: This is going to be pretty similar to what Menolith said , but it comes down to the brain . Human hearts can be stopped and started , organs can fail and be replaced , but the brain is the big one . If a heart stops beating , no fresh blood gets to the brain , which means no oxygen . After so long without oxygen , brain cells start to freak out , and die . When a person has a certain type of stroke , starving their brain of oxygen , those brains cells will self - destruct as well ( after doing this weird thing where they start firing like crazy first ... ) . Similarly , lots of illness and organ failures , etc , will kill a person by depriving them of oxygen or brain function , in a weird way . Pneumonia , toxins poisoning the blood , anything that causes cardia arrest , massive organ failure , etc .... eventually , the brain stops working . Now , as to why we ca n't revive it : those brain cells die . It 's not a matter of turning on a switch and lighting the brain back up ( like we can sort of do with a heart ) . It 's that what we would want to turn on and light back up is n't there anymore . Think of a tree 's leaf -- in the autumn , it shrivels and falls . If brain cells are like leaves , then when they shrivel and fall , putting more water on the tree wo n't bring those leaves back . The difference is that trees can grow more leaves -- we ca n't grow new brain cells . Please let me know if I can make it more clear :) I can try to find some sources to back up some of the information if you want too .
label: human

The one you can carry might be doable briefly , if you ran with it . The rock you can heft you can either Chuck to hit that speed or run with , and the pebble you can throw . The ship of the line was usually the largest and most powerful ship in a navy's fleet, and it was used to lead the other ships into battle.

One of the things that made a ship of the line different from other ships is that it had a lot of guns mounted on its sides. These guns were used to fire at other ships and try to sink them during naval battles. The ship of the line also had a lot of armor to protect it from enemy fire.

Another thing that made a ship of the line different is that it was very slow and difficult to maneuver. This made it hard for the ship to change direction quickly, but it also made it very sturdy and able to withstand a lot of damage.

text:
There are many complex factors that have contributed to conflicts, corruption, and other challenges on the African continent. Here are a few possible reasons:

Colonization and the legacy of colonialism: Many African countries were colonized by European powers, which often imposed their own systems and governments on the countries they conquered. This often led to the exploitation of natural resources and the suppression of local cultures and traditions. The legacy of colonialism has had a lasting impact on African countries, and many have struggled to build strong, stable governments in the post-colonial era.

Poverty and economic inequality: Many African countries have high levels of poverty and economic inequality, which can lead to conflict and unrest. When people are struggling to meet their basic needs, they may be more likely to turn to violence or other forms of conflict as a means of survival.

Ethnic and religious differences: Some African countries have a history of ethnic and religious tensions, which can lead to conflict. When different groups within a country do not get along or feel that they are being treated unfairly, this can lead to violence and unrest.

Weak governments and corruption: In some African countries, the government may be weak or ineffective, which can lead to corruption and other problems. When the government is not able to effectively provide basic services or protect the rights of its citizens, this can create an environment in which conflict is more likely to occur.

Natural disasters and environmental challenges: African countries also face a number of natural disasters and environmental challenges, such as drought, famine, and disease outbreaks, which can contribute to conflict and instability.

These are just a few of the many complex factors that have contributed to the challenges facing the African continent. It's important to remember that every country is different, and each has its own unique set of challenges and opportunities.

This device, known as the "lubricator cup," was patented in 1872 and became very popular with railroads and other industries that used steam engines. text:
It's true that China is a major player in the global market for manufactured goods, including cars and cellphones. However, it is not accurate to say that China routinely copies products without getting in trouble or infringing on patents.

In fact, there are laws in place that protect intellectual property, including patents, trademarks, and copyrights. These laws apply to companies and individuals in China, just as they do in other countries. If a company in China were to copy a product without permission, they could be sued for infringing on the patents or other intellectual property rights of the original manufacturer.

The World Trade Organization (WTO) is an international organization that helps to regulate international trade. One of the ways it does this is by establishing rules for how countries should protect intellectual property. These rules are designed to ensure that companies and individuals who create new products or inventions are able to benefit from their work, and to encourage innovation.

So, while China is a major player in the global market for manufactured goods, it is not accurate to say that they are able to freely copy products without consequences. Instead, China, like other countries, has laws in place to protect intellectual property and follows international rules established by the WTO to ensure fair competition in the global market.

text:
Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a group of chronic diseases that cause inflammation in the digestive tract. The two main types of IBD are Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.



Crohn's disease can affect any part of the digestive tract, from the mouth to the anus, but it most commonly affects the small intestine and colon. Ulcerative colitis only affects the colon, also known as the large intestine.



Symptoms of IBD may include abdominal pain, diarrhea, rectal bleeding, weight loss, and fatigue. The severity of symptoms can vary widely from person to person and may come and go in cycles.



The exact cause of IBD is unknown, but it is thought to be a combination of genetic, environmental, and immune system factors. IBD is not the same as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which is a common gastrointestinal disorder that does not involve inflammation.



Treatment for IBD may include medications to reduce inflammation, such as corticosteroids or immune system suppressors, as well as dietary changes and surgery. It is important for people with IBD to work with a healthcare team to develop a treatment plan that is appropriate for their individual needs.

text:
why is it not possible for an overweight person to stop eating for a while and just live off their bodyfat for a while ? I mean , is n't the whole reason your body stores fat to use it for energy ? Explain like I'm five.
This content may violate our content policy. If you believe this to be in error, please submit your feedback — your input will aid our research in this area.
Sure! When you eat food, your body breaks it down into energy that it can use to keep you going. Some of this energy is used right away, and some of it is stored in your body for later use. When you're trying to lose weight, your body uses the stored energy (also known as fat) to help you do things like walk, run, and play.

However, it's not a good idea to stop eating completely, because your body needs food to stay healthy and strong. Without enough food, your body will start to use up the stored energy very quickly, and you might start to feel tired, weak, or sick.

It's much better to try to eat healthy foods in the right amounts, and to get plenty of exercise, so that your body can use the stored energy in a healthy way. This will help you lose weight slowly and safely, and it will also help you feel better overall.

East Germany, on the other hand, was a communist country that was controlled by the Soviet Union (a country in Europe that was led by a group of people called communists). The Berlin Wall was made of concrete and was very tall, so it was very difficult for people to climb over it or dig under it. It also had guards who watched over it to make sure no one tried to escape. The Berlin Wall stood for almost 30 years, until it was finally torn down in the year 1989.

text: There are three things to consider when thinking about dissolution . The solvent ’s ( water ’s ) interaction with itself ( among nearby water molecules ) , the solute ’s ( salt or sugar ’s ) interaction with itself , and the interaction between the solvent and the solute . In order to dissolve , this last interaction must be strong enough to overcome the first two . If this is the case , then the salt / sugar ions / molecules can break out of the rigid structure they assume in the solid state , and water molecules can move to surround them , forming stabilizing interactions . So what ’s basically happening is the solute molecules are becoming surrounded by water molecules in such a way that they can move around freely like a liquid rather than being “ locked ” in the solid state , which is much more rigid . And solutes wo n’t evaporate with water unless they have a high vapor pressure or low boiling point . You can leave some salt or sugar water out in the sun for a while and you ’ll end up with a container of salt or sugar crystals .

text: Imagine you have a cardboard box of 1 cubic metre . You put it on a scale and it weighs 1 kg . You then invite a journalist to report about your cardboard box , which is awesome . You tell the journaliast that it weighs 1 kg and measures 1 meter in every direction . The journalist then writes an article titled " Cardboard box discovered that is lighter than air " , because the cardboard box only weighs 1 kg , and a cubic metre of air weighs 1,3 kg . The headline ignores the fact that the cardboard box is itself filled with air , that also weighs something . However , air normally does not show up on a scale so the air in the cardboard box is ignored . The headline thus comes from comparing apples to oranges : a weight on a scale while ignoring any air is compared to the weight of air , which does not even show up on a scale .

text:
Insider trading is illegal because it involves buying or selling a company's stock based on information that is not publicly available. This is unfair to other investors who don't have access to this information and can't make informed decisions about whether to buy or sell the stock. 
Imagine that you and your friend both want to buy a toy at the store, but your friend knows that the store is about to have a big sale and the toy will be much cheaper tomorrow. If your friend buys the toy today and sells it to you tomorrow at the sale price, they will make a profit while you will have paid full price. This isn't fair, because your friend had access to information that you didn't have, and they used that information to their advantage. 
In the stock market, insider trading is illegal because it's similar to this situation. When someone has inside information about a company and they buy or sell the company's stock based on that information, they have an unfair advantage over other investors. This can harm the integrity of the stock market and undermine public trust in it. That's why insider trading is illegal.

text:
Good Friday is a Christian holiday commemorating the Crucifixion of Jesus Christ and his death at Calvary. It is observed during Holy Week, the week before Easter Sunday, and is a time of fasting, repentance, and prayer for many Christians.

In the Christian tradition, Good Friday is considered a day of great significance because it marks the day that Jesus, the Son of God, willingly gave up his life for the salvation of humanity. Christians believe that through his death and resurrection, Jesus conquered death and made it possible for all people to have eternal life.

Good Friday is observed in many different ways by Christians around the world. Some may attend church services, participate in prayer vigils, or engage in other forms of devotion and reflection. Many Christian denominations also observe Good Friday as a day of fasting, in which believers abstain from certain foods or activities as a way of remembering Jesus' sacrifice and demonstrating their own devotion to God.

Regardless of how it is observed, Good Friday is an important holiday for many Christians, as it serves as a reminder of the great love and sacrifice of Jesus for humanity.

text: The network is deployed out in segments . Think of it like this - each floor of each building gets its own network , and then the buildings have routers that connect all of those networks together , and then those routers connect to the campus ' data center where there are bigger routers that handle connecting not only the buildings to each other but the entire campus to the Internet at large . As far as the WiFi goes - when you 're deploying a campus - wide network , there will be centralized management of the access points in order to handle things like channel assignments and client management . Every access point is given the same name and to a user looks like seamless coverage , but there will be dozens if not hundreds or thousands of access points blanketing the campus with the same SSID . However , those access points are all going to be working on different channels so as to not overlap signals with each other ( either in the general 1 - 6 - 11 configuration to eliminate channel overlap or in a 1 - 4 - 8 - 11 configuration to simply minimize it ) . As well , the central management of the access points will work to try and prevent a single access point from getting overloaded . Throw 200 people into a lecture hall with one access point and the amount of effective bandwidth for users is going to be quite minimal . But if you have ten access points in and around the lecture hall , the server can force clients to disconnect from one access point and reconnect to another access point with a decent signal strength so that you have people on the edges of the hall connecting to access points in other rooms so that each access point gets 20 users competing for the bandwidth instead of 200 . Access points can be connected in two ways - wired or mesh . If an access point is wired , as most access points in a campus - level network should be , it will have its own dedicated bandwidth that it can serve of either 100 Mbps or 1 Gbps . If the access point is connected in a mesh configuration , bandwidth management becomes more complex because the access point needs to use its radios for both serving clients and getting their traffic upstream . Why does it work better in some places rather than others ? There are a lot of reasons for that . It could be that other users are causing bandwidth congestion - a couple of people connected to the same access point are watching Netflix , so you go to download a file and find that the pool of bandwidth you 're sharing is mostly going to serve them instead of you because their usage is more constant than yours . It could be that there are too many users connected to the access point - wireless bandwidth is a shared pool among the users , so the more users there are the more contention there is to try and use the same bandwidth and the more overhead increases trying to separate everyone 's traffic . And it could be that you 're getting a bad signal - your radio might indicate that you 're getting a good signal power , but the wall between you and the access point might be scattering that signal to hell and back in a way that causes both your device and the access point to have to constantly resend data to ensure it 's being received properly .

text: For the sake of doing this like an ELI5 we will focus on the .coms rather than going into detail on all of the different structures for obscure TLD ( Top level domains ) like .ms , .tv , ... Essentially the companies you buy a domain from ( eg Godaddy - Registrar ) need to speculate on how many domains they will sell per year , and pay that number multiplied by a registration fee ( 7 - 10 $ ) to the larger body ( eg Verisign - Registry ) . So it takes the financial power of a large company to pay the up front cost to the registry . Additionally the Registrars need to follow specific rules and regulations laid out by ICANN ( the regulating body of the industry ) and other financial / procedural standards . Bonus fact : At the origin of the .com , there was only one company ( Network Solutions ) who was both the sole registrar , and the registry . This was later broken up , Verisign essentially purchasing the Registry power , and allowing other companies to set up Registrar functions . Source : Worked for a Domain registrar for a few years .

In the meantime, there are some things you can do to help alleviate your symptoms:

Use over-the-counter pain medication, such as acetaminophen (Tylenol) or ibuprofen (Advil) to help with the headache.

Try using a saline nasal spray to help moisten your nasal passages and reduce congestion.

Drink plenty of fluids, such as water and clear soups, to help thin out mucus and promote sinus drainage.

Use a humidifier to add moisture to the air and help reduce congestion.

If your symptoms persist or worsen, it is important to see a doctor for further evaluation and treatment. A deviated septum could cause these symptoms, and a doctor will be able to determine the cause of your symptoms and recommend the appropriate treatment.

text:
In logic, a quantifier is a symbol or word that specifies the quantity or extent of the elements in the domain of a logical statement. There are two types of quantifiers: universal quantifiers and existential quantifiers.



A universal quantifier is a symbol or word that specifies that a particular statement holds for all elements in the domain of the statement. For example, the statement "For all x, x is a number" is a universal statement because it asserts that the property of being a number holds for every element in the domain of x. In symbolic logic, the universal quantifier is usually represented by the symbol "∀".



An existential quantifier is a symbol or word that specifies that there exists at least one element in the domain of a logical statement that satisfies a particular condition. For example, the statement "There exists an x such that x is a prime number" is an existential statement because it asserts that there is at least one element in the domain of x that is a prime number. In symbolic logic, the existential quantifier is usually represented by the symbol "∃".



Quantifiers play a crucial role in formal logic and are used to make more complex statements about the relationships between different elements in a logical domain. They are used to specify the extent to which a particular property holds for a given set of elements, and can be combined with other logical operators to form more complex statements.

text: I'm an Aussie and I purchased 5 of these properties from 2008 to 2010. I was looking for positive cash flow on properties for not too much upfront investment. The USA property market made sense because of the high Aussie $$ at the time, the depressed property market in the US and the expensive market here.  I used an investment web-site that allowed me to screen properties by yield and after eliminating outliers, went for the city with the highest consistent yield performance. I settled on Toledo, Ohio as it had the highest yields and was severely impacted by the housing crisis. I bought my first property for $18K US which was a little over $17K AUD.  The property was a duplex in great condition in a reasonable location.  Monthly rentals $US900 and rents guaranteed and direct deposited into my bank account every month by section 8.  Taxes $900 a year and $450 a year for water. Total return around $US8,000.   My second property was a short sale in a reasonable area. The asking was $US8K and was a single family in good condition already tenanted.  I went through the steps with the bank and after a few months, was the proud owner of another tenanted, positive cash flow property returning $600 a month gross. Taxes of $600 a year and water about the same.  $US6K NET a year on a property that cost $AUD8K Third and fourth were two single family dwellings in good areas.  These both cost $US14K each and returned $US700 a month each.  $US28K for two properties that gross around $US15K a year.   My fifth property was a tax foreclosure of a guy with 2 kids whose wife had left him and whose friend had stolen the money to repay the property taxes.  He was basically on the bones of his butt and was staring down the barrel of being homeless with two kids.  The property was in great condition in a reasonable part of town.  The property cost me $4K.  I signed up the previous owner in a land contract to buy his house back for $US30K. Payments over 10 years at 7% came out to around $US333 per month.  I made him an offer whereby if he acted as my property manager, i would forgo the land contract payments and pay him a percentage of the rents in exchange for his services. text: Devision by a negative number may be strange when you consider dividing as literally dividing numbers . As an example 6 / 2 would mean you make two piles of 3 . Thus the answer is 3 . This conceptualization does n't function for negative numbers , but it is by no means the only way we can think about division . It is n't even the best way . Division is ( for this discussion at least ) best thought of as simply the inverse of multiplication so ( 6 * 2 ) / 2 = 6 or more generally ( x * y)/y = x. For all values of y other than 0 choosing a different x changes the value of x * y but when y is 0 x * y must always be 0 . This would imply that 0 is the only number that can be divided by 0 . Examining 0/0 we find flaws with it too . We 're trying to remove the multiplication of a 0 . But any information about what the other number was was lost when it was multiplied with 0 .
label: human

text:
Net neutrality is the idea that all websites and internet services should be treated equally by internet service providers (ISPs). This means that an ISP should not be able to block or slow down access to any particular website or service, or charge more for access to some websites and less for access to others.

If the internet is not neutral, then ISPs could do things like block access to certain websites, slow down access to certain websites (making them load more slowly), or charge more money to access certain websites. This could make it hard for people to see and use the websites they want to use, and it could also make it harder for new websites and companies to compete with established ones.

For example, without net neutrality, an ISP might block access to a social media site that competes with a site they own, or they might slow down access to a streaming video service that competes with a service they offer. This would give their own sites and services an unfair advantage, and make it hard for other sites and services to compete.

Overall, a world without net neutrality could be a less free and open place, where it is harder for people to access the information and services they want, and where it is harder for new ideas and companies to succeed.
label: ai

text:
A filibuster is a tactic used by members of the United States Senate to delay or block a vote on a bill or resolution. It is often used by the minority party (the party that does not have a majority of seats in the Senate) to try to prevent the majority party from passing legislation.

Filibusters can be ended by a process called "cloture," which requires three-fifths of the Senators (60 out of 100) to vote to end the filibuster and proceed to a vote on the legislation. Cloture is difficult to achieve, and it can take a long time to get enough votes to end a filibuster. This means that if a filibuster is successful, it can effectively kill a bill or resolution, even if a majority of Senators support it.

You may be hearing more about filibusters now because they have been used more frequently in recent years, and some people believe that they are being used too often and are blocking important legislation from being passed. There has been a lot of debate about whether the filibuster should be reformed or eliminated altogether.

In other societies, individuals may have been chosen to be the leader of a group or community based on their personal qualities, such as their wisdom, courage, or ability to make fair decisions.

One way that individuals could become rulers was through a process called "ascension," where they inherited the position of king or queen from their parent or another relative. This is often called a monarchy, and the rulers are known as monarchs. In other societies, leaders may be chosen by a council of elders or through a democratic process, where the people in the community get to vote for their leader.

In some societies, the process for becoming a ruler was not always peaceful, and individuals might have had to fight or compete for the position. For example, in ancient times, it was common for people to wage war in order to conquer other lands and become the ruler of those territories.

Overall, the process for becoming a ruler or member of the royal family can vary greatly depending on the specific society and its cultural traditions.

There are 338 MPs in Canada, with each province having a certain number of seats based on its population.

In summary, the provinces in Canada are like the states in the United States in that they have their own governments and are responsible for certain areas of governance. text: Radiation is a type of energy that is emitted by certain materials, such as radioactive elements. It is possible to feel the effects of radiation, but it depends on the type of radiation and the amount of exposure.Alpha radiation is made up of alpha particles, which are made up of two protons and two neutrons. Alpha particles are not very penetrating, which means they do not travel very far through matter. If you were to touch something that was emitting alpha radiation, you might feel a slight warmth or tingling sensation. However, alpha particles are not very harmful because they can be stopped by a sheet of paper or a few centimeters of air.Beta radiation is made up of beta particles, which are high-energy electrons or positrons. Beta particles are more penetrating than alpha particles and can travel through a few centimeters of wood or a few millimeters of aluminum. If you were to touch something that was emitting beta radiation, you might feel a slight burn or radiation sickness. Beta particles can be harmful if you are exposed to them for a long time or if you receive a high dose of radiation.Gamma radiation is made up of high-energy photons, which are particles of light. Gamma radiation is very penetrating and can pass through several centimeters of lead or several meters of concrete. If you were to touch something that was emitting gamma radiation, you might not feel anything at all, because gamma radiation does not interact with the body in the same way that alpha or beta particles do. Gamma radiation can be harmful if you receive a high dose of radiation, but it is not possible to feel the difference between alpha, beta, and gamma radiation just by touch.

text: Real estate and construction , as well as necessity . Think about it this way . Most hospitals are big , sprawling centers with lots of parking ( and possibly garages ) , all dedicated primarily to emergency and intensive care needs . Most clinics / doctor 's offices are typically smaller buildings either in an already established medical park or in any kind of commercial real estate , really . Putting stuff like dentists , optometrists , etc .. all in the hospital as well increases your real estate and construction needs , and adds more strain on the infrastructure of the hospital itself as well . It makes more financial sense to have the less important practices in smaller offices pretty much " wherever works " because it cuts down on costs and requirements for the facility . There 's also typically little overlap in the needs of intensive care patients ( ER , ICU , burn wards , etc ) and routine maintenance ( dental work , eye exams , general medicine ) , whereas the practices within the hospital can have a fair bit of overlap and urgency . For instance , somebody comes in to the ER for something and may need to be transferred to another department quickly . It makes sense for those practices to be close together , like in the same building . that said , some places to have fairly centralized practices . Kaiser Permanente in my area has three main medical parks and does a number of general medicine and prescription stuff right at their main hospital . Another reason , Hospitals are typically owned by the health group themselves ( Kaiser , Sutter , etc ) whereas a dentist 's practice may be owned independently but work as a partner to the medical group . This is also why you see some places that take multiple forms of insurance ( like Kaiser and Blue Shield for the same practice ) .

So, to use a semicolon correctly, you need to have two independent clauses in your sentence that you want to connect. text:
Airline pilots can predict turbulence by using a variety of tools and techniques. Here are a few ways they can do this:

Weather forecasts: Pilots can check weather forecasts and maps to see if there are any areas of stormy weather or strong winds that could cause turbulence.

Radar: Many planes are equipped with radar systems that can detect areas of turbulence ahead of the plane. The radar sends out a beam of energy and measures how it bounces back, which can help pilots see if there are any areas of rough air ahead.

Other pilots: Pilots can also communicate with other pilots who have recently flown through the same area to get reports on the conditions they experienced.

Experience: Pilots who have flown for a long time may have a good sense of what types of weather and conditions are likely to cause turbulence, based on their own past experiences.

Overall, pilots use a combination of these tools and techniques to try to predict and avoid turbulence as much as possible. However, it is important to note that turbulence is a natural occurrence and can sometimes be difficult to predict, especially if it is caused by rapidly changing weather conditions.

Classic liberals believe in a free market economy and the protection of individual rights, but they also support a limited role for the government in regulating the economy and protecting individual freedoms.Libertarianism is a political ideology that emphasizes individual freedom and a limited government. Libertarians believe in a free market economy and the protection of individual rights, and they generally oppose government regulation and intervention in people's lives.Independent is a term that can refer to someone who does not align with a particular political party or ideology. Genetically modified (GM) food is food that has been produced using techniques of genetic engineering, which allow scientists to modify the characteristics of plants and animals in ways that are not possible through traditional breeding methods. GM food has been the subject of much debate and controversy, with some people expressing concerns about the safety of these products and the potential impacts on the environment.

One concern about GM food is that it might be harmful to people who eat it. Some people worry that the genetic modifications made to these foods could have unintended consequences, such as causing allergic reactions or other health problems. However, many scientific studies have been conducted to assess the safety of GM foods, and the overwhelming majority of these studies have concluded that GM foods are safe for human consumption.

Another concern about GM food is that it might have negative impacts on the environment. For example, some people worry that GM crops could cross-breed with non-GM crops or wild plants, leading to the spread of genetically modified traits. There is also concern that the use of GM crops could lead to the development of herbicide-resistant weeds, which could be difficult to control.

Despite these concerns, many scientists and organizations, including the World Health Organization, believe that GM foods have the potential to offer significant benefits, including increased crop yields, improved resistance to pests and diseases, and increased food security in developing countries.

text: Karl Marx was a philosopher, economist, and sociologist who lived in the 19th century. text: In osmosis , water travels through a membrane from a low - concentration ( i.e. less dissolved solids ) mixture to a high - concentration mixture until the concentration on both sides is the same . This happens spontaneously and can be quite dramatic if one side is extremely highly concentrated . This is why drinking salt water is so harmful , osmotic pressure starts sucking water out of your cells . * Reverse * osmosis is the inverse process , forcing water to cross from the concentrated side to the pure side . This * wo n't * happen naturally and requires high pressures , but also acts as a very effective filtration process because the membrane excludes most particulates and large ions .

text: Self-plagiarism is the act of reusing your own work or ideas without properly citing yourself as the source. This can be a problem because it can mislead readers into thinking that the reused work or ideas are new and original, when in fact they are not.Imagine that you wrote a paper for a class and received a good grade. Later, you decide to use some of the same ideas or text from that paper in a new assignment for a different class. If you don't properly cite the original paper as the source of those ideas or text, you are committing self-plagiarism.It's important to properly cite your own work and give credit to yourself when you reuse your own ideas or text. This helps to ensure that you are being honest and transparent about the sources of your work and helps readers to understand where your ideas came from.
